summary: Replacement client_engineer writer started because the original writer briefly produced allowed-path local UI/test diff but did not respond to repeated wait/status/interruption requests and emitted no REVIEW/PARTIAL handoff. After the original writer was closed, those transient uncommitted business diffs were no longer present in the main worktree, so the replacement starts from the current branch checkpoint and must produce a fresh reviewable diff, tests, and handoff.

tests_run.result: Original writer diff was observed before close, but no REVIEW/PARTIAL handoff or commit was produced; after close, main worktree showed only process-file changes.

decisions: Close the unresponsive original writer after repeated wait, status request, and interrupt收口指令 produced no handoff.
Do not trust or claim uncommitted transient writer diff as an artifact; replacement must create a fresh reviewable diff from the current branch checkpoint.
Use a replacement client_engineer to implement, test, and emit the required REVIEW handoff without expanding scope.
Do not mark the task ACCEPTED/CLOSED until independent qa_reviewer accepts and PR CI/main integration complete.
risks: The first writer left no auditable implementation handoff, so any earlier transient diff is treated as non-authoritative.
Replacement must keep changes inside allowed paths and must not introduce new dependencies, telemetry, accounts, Electron IPC, or authoritative client state.
requested_action: Create a fresh allowed-path accessibility diff from the current branch checkpoint, run focused tests, update task status to REVIEW, write REVIEW handoff to qa_reviewer, and commit.
